topics.title: Introduction to the Course
topics.summary: The instructor introduces herself and welcomes students to the web development course. She has taught over a million students worldwide and is the lead instructor at the London App Brewery.

topics.title: Course Content and Structure
topics.summary: The course offers over 55 hours of HD video content, step-by-step tutorials, and interactive coding exercises, covering every aspect of web development from HTML to React. It is project-based, allowing students to learn by building real-world websites and web applications.

topics.title: Course Projects and Outcomes
topics.summary: Students will build various projects, including a product website, games, and a to-do list app, using HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, Node, React, and the MERN stack. These projects are suitable for showing off at job interviews.

topics.title: Target Audience and Prerequisites
topics.summary: The course assumes no prior programming experience and is suitable for complete beginners. Advanced developers can start at a level that suits them best by reviewing the curriculum.
key_points: Advanced developers can start at their level

topics.title: Course Value and Testimonials
topics.summary: The course offers the same curriculum as a 12-week in-person programming bootcamp, but at a lower cost and with more flexibility. Past students have rated the course highly, with over a million students having rated it five stars.
